rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Walk-away test (M2+)
50% magic rule (M3-M8)
Trust the agentic design — no human backstops
Exercise quality ladder
(moved to cross_module)
One exercise = one bounded activity
Modules are composable across training variants
Three-level completeness
Pattern-recognition LOs need n>=2 instances
Progression-with-variations is the training's spine
Student agency — adults decide
Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens
Split a mega-exercise when phases are distinct skills
Artifact-realism check
Teach the principle and the technique — default
Auto-fire sim + eval after significant rewrite
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block already flags judges INVALIDATED by the 2026-05-24 reshape and re-audit pending — this judge run is part of that re-fire.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions stay in prompts, not body prose
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Step 5 forcing functions live inside fenced prompts (screen-and-ready, one-at-a-time); body sentence 'push back if it dumps all four criteria at once. One bug at a time' is steering/recovery callout adjacent to prompt, with a dedicated fallback prompt — correct prompt-layer placement.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Corporate infra is nice-to-have, never a forcing function
Personal-first, team promotion via human conversation
Measure silent failure, not just friction
Don't pre-set categories before the experiment runs
Verification layers sample different error distributions
Cheap-before-expensive verification-ladder ordering
A rule in memory that does not force is worse than no rule
Short skeptical pushbacks are re-verify triggers
Cohort-delivered exercises run on trainer-paced concurrent execution
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prework runs pre-cohort/self-paced by design; 30 min target in maintainer-block is per-student, consistent with concurrent model.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Host-skill is optional; default cohort is no host-skill
Reflection beats prefer in-chat over file-write
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Closing instruction 'Write it down, file/line, or a one-sentence summary in a note' is student-owned external memory hand-off to M1, not a curriculum-mandated file-write or proof-of-reflection artifact.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Classroom is the default delivery mode; body teaches the work
(moved to cross_module)
Eval is a fixed yardstick
Reading burden for verification is banned
Self-report and self-audit charity
Offered structures need a forced-engagement mechanic
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Step 5 attaches forced-engagement to screening ('push back if it dumps all four criteria at once') plus a fallback fenced one-at-a-time prompt — real engagement, not rubber-stamp.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-type blindness on ingestion
Name both floor AND ceiling when task selection drives outcome
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Both floor and ceiling named for the trivial bug ('a few lines here and there... not a multi-file refactor') in Step 5; the four task sizes at top each carry shape constraints. Anti-pattern callouts present in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Maintainer-block hedges that name the section's own failure mode are a cut-signal
Audience tier determines authoring substrate
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Step 5 verifies 'the repo can test, branch, commit, and open a PR' — smoke-test of M1 readiness before M1 depends on it. Step 2 also smoke-tests Claude Code session start.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers as staged progression
(moved to cross_module)
Removals are strategic — ask before cutting
(moved to cross_module)
Symmetric visible-mechanics flow > runtime fork
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Step 3 ships symmetric agentic-fetch + manual-download paths (both land same tarball, Steps 4-5 identical after). Maintainer-block names the design choice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Load-bearing platform feature gets no fallback
Plug points + UX
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prework has no Plug Points section (N/A axis). UX axis: prompts pasteable, paths discoverable, hand-off to M1 explicit ('the bug needs to survive into M1').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Eval / judge architecture
Per-exercise leap test
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prework, not exercise — leap-test rule fires per-exercise. Artefact-contracts table covers the equivalent hand-off discipline.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: (moved to cross_module)
Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block names install-blocker failure mode + non-fatal escape ('Curated skills first fire at M3, so a failed Step-4 install is repairable... Rescue paths in trainer-guide.md'). Step 5 body names everything-at-once failure mode + one-at-a-time escape prompt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Heavy fan-out prompts get a body-side recovery callout
Save belongs with authoring, not loading
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Skills install closes at Step 4 (the create/setup beat for this prework). M3 (the use module) opens with skill invocation, not install — correct save-with-authoring shape.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Convenience-driven prompts with known shape-bias get body-side acknowledgment callouts
Deliberate-antipattern prompts are class-judge exempt
Session widget — source shape; place by learning flow
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Step 2 carries `**Session** *(new, "prework")*` at step top — canonical source shape. Single session for whole prework — fits.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pre-action framing blocks don't preview the prompt's payoff
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Step 3 names the artifact; Step 4 names activity + constraint ('Mechanical setup. Claude needs to write outside the repo... approve the permission prompts'); Step 5 names activity ('Claude walks three trivial bug candidates... helps you pick one, then confirms the repo can test, branch, commit, and open a PR'). No payoff/reveal previews.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: (moved to cross_module)
(moved to cross_module)
Engineer-audience audit fixes default to body callout
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Step 5 'push back if it dumps all four criteria at once' is body-callout shape; one-at-a-time fenced prompt is optional escalation — aligns with engineer-audience default (recoverable miss, recovery surface present).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Multi-session (or any new mechanic mid-arc) ships as packaging
/clear is a tool with a purpose, not a ceremony
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body closes with 'Close the prework session when Claude confirms readiness. Module 1 opens fresh in the same repo.' Session-close named explicitly, no /clear ceremony — correct two-question diagnostic (window is closing anyway, M1 opens fresh).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inbound links preserve their pedagogical beat across reference restructuring
Scan at the unit the rule names
Verify the existing mechanism before proposing surgery
Callouts next to prompts: steer, don't pre-empt
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Step 5 callouts ('push back if it dumps all four criteria at once', 'If Claude's screening feels like everything-at-once, ask Claude to use the AskUserQuestion tool, or to give you the candidate-bug screen one bug at a time. Your call.') are conditional-on-observation steering shapes, not pre-emption against a specific named failure mode the prompt mechanics already prevent. Notice-and-recover pattern, generalises across causes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Persistent TODO surfaces are stale by default
Maintainer blocks point, they don't restate — and link the architecture doc where they touch delivery
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block points correctly: 'Delivery architecture... canonical in training-architecture.md §Working directory model / §Material distribution / §Skills / §Rule files. Not restated here.' Packaging row points to build-script header + training-architecture.md §Material distribution. Pure-pointer discipline applied to cross-cutting facts; unique in-file content (runtime budget, transport, CONTENT_URL placeholder design, artefact-contracts table) stays in-block.
